definition: a backup is a copy of a piece of information on a computer that you can use if the original is lost or damaged
example: remember to make a backup copy of the file
it’s always a good idea to back up any important documents

definition: if you back up a piece of information on a computer, you make a copy of it so that you can use it if the original is lost or damaged
example: it’s always a good idea to back up any important documents

definition: a cable is a thick wire covered in plastic that carries electricity or digital information
example: she tripped over a loose cable

definition: if information comes up on a computer screen, it appears
example: move the mouse over the image and the instructions should come up

definition: a desk is a place where you can get help or information, for example in an airport or a hotel
example: I’m sorry, I can’t help you – have you tried the information desk?

definition: a desktop is the background of a computer screen which shows icons (small pictures) of programs that can be used
example: have you tried downloading it to your desktop and seeing if you can open it from there?

definition: something that is external is on the outside or comes from outside an object, building, organisation, etc.
example: the computer has an external hard drive
the project is externally financed
internal walls

definition: a file is a set of information with a name on a computer
example: I can’t open the file

definition: a folder is a group of files stored together on a computer
example: are you sure you’re looking in the right folder?

definition: if you free or free up someone or something, you make them available to be used for something
example: you ought to delete those old files to free some space
collocations: free (up) someone or something to do something

example: is this chair free?

definition: an icon is a small picture on a computer screen which opens a particular program when you press on it with a computer mouse or touch it with your finger
example: click on the printer icon and wait a few seconds

definition: a keyboard is a piece of equipment that is used to put information into a computer. Keys are the letters and numbers that you press on a keyboard
example: be careful not to spill coffee over the keyboard
select the file and press the delete key

definition: if you knock something, you touch or hit it by accident, especially so that it moves or falls
example: Rob tripped on a wire and managed to knock the whole computer over

definition: if you say that someone or something is an offender, you think that they are causing a problem
example: people often forget to lock this cupboard and I’m afraid you’re one of the worst offenders!

definition: a password is a secret group of letters or numbers that you type into a computer system or electronic device so that you can use it
example: I can’t access my account because I’ve forgotten the password

definition: protection is the process of keeping someone or something safe from harm or damage
example: security software will give you greater protection from viruses
what can we do to protect ourselves against heart disease?
collocations: protect someone or something against/from something

definition: if you reset a machine or device, you make changes so that it will work again or work in a different way
example: I’ll reset your password and send you a new one in a minute
your password reset was successful

definition: if a computer disk is scanned, a computer program checks it to make sure that it does not contain viruses (programs that destroy information)
example: the disk was scanned for viruses before we started using it
it could be a virus - you need to run a security scan

definition: a scanner is a piece of equipment that is used for creating an electronic picture of something on a computer
example: place the document on the scanner

definition: if you select something, you choose it from a group of things. If you select something on a computer screen, you choose it from a list of choices
example: select ‘open’ and then ‘edit file’

definition: a campaign is a series of things you say or do because you want to persuade people about something. A social media campaign is a campaign that uses new technology like Facebook and Twitter to communicate this information
example: my boss wants me to run our social media campaign

definition: a socket is a place with holes in a wall or machine where you can connect electrical equipment
example: there’s a socket behind you on the wall

definition: if you trip, your foot hits something by accident and you fall or lose your balance
example: it’s really slippery so be careful you don’t trip!
it was only a little trip but she broke her arm badly

definition: to advance means to move forward and towards something or someone
example: if you can match all the images, then you advance to the next level
collocations: advance on somewhere Collocates:  on the black market

definition: if you clear a place or clear something from it, you remove things that are not wanted there
example: can you clear the glasses from the tables?

definition: comfort is when you feel physically relaxed and happy because you are in a pleasant place
example: you can shop from the comfort of your own home
a warm and comfortable bed
careful design is needed to minimise user discomfort
these shoes are really uncomfortable

definition: a consumer is a person who buys and uses goods and services
example: consumer rights
she explained the whole thing in great detail

definition: if something is endless, it seems to have no end or limit
example: the possibilities are endless
women have fought endlessly for equality and fair treatment

definition: an enemy is someone who is opposed to you and may try to cause problems for you or harm you
example: an enemy soldier

definition: to enhance something means to improve it or make it look more attractive
example: this skill will greatly enhance your musical creativity
skill development and performance enhancement are difficult tasks

definition: if you experience a particular feeling, situation, or problem, it happens to you or affects you
example: it allows you to experience the daily life of a farmer
a lot of people we’ve spoken to have had a similar experience

definition: a fantasy is a pleasant state or situation that is not true or real
example: the idea of living in a castle is just pure fantasy

definition: gaming is the activity of playing computer games. Someone who plays computer games very regularly is a gamer
example: do you have any hobbies outside of gaming?
Tom has always been a keen gamer

definition: if you gather things, you bring them together so that you can use them
example: you gather resources to build your own city

definition: household means connected with a house or flat and all the people living in it
example: I’m working for a company that sells household goods

definition: if two or more people interact, or if one person interacts with another, they communicate and react to each other while doing an activity together
example: you can play on your own or interact with other players
I enjoy the interaction with students from other courses
teaching is done in small, interactive workshops

definition: a machine is a piece of equipment that does a particular kind of work. Machinery is a word used to refer to machines in general, especially in a factory or on a farm, etc
example: modern machinery and advanced technology assures quality control
a coffee machine

definition: a mission is an important piece of work that a person or group of people is given to do, especially one that involves travelling
example: he was sent on a diplomatic mission to South Africa

definition: a modification is a small change to something, especially in order to improve it
example: modification of the engine is required
collocations: modification of/to something Collocates:  obsessed by/with something or someone

definition: if you modify something, you change it slightly in order to improve it
example: they’ve agreed to modify their returns policy to make exchanging goods easier

definition: over something
example: she had an obsession with cleanliness
collocations: an obsession with something or someone

definition: over something
example: she’s obsessive about the state of the house

definition: over something
example: she had an obsession with cleanliness

definition: an occupation is a job. This word is used in forms and formal writing
example: please supply your name, address and occupation

definition: an opponent is someone who is competing against you
example: he received a much greater percentage of the vote than his opponent
recovery of stolen goods

definition: if you release something or someone, you allow them to leave a place where they have been kept. If you release a film, book, game, song, etc, you make it available for people to see or buy for the first time
example: the band have just released their third album
the film is on general release next Friday

definition: something or someone that is remarkable is unusual in a way which makes people surprised or impressed
example: the computer and video games industry has experienced remarkable growth
Mr Galvin was remarkably successful in the local elections

definition: a resource is something that you can use to help you achieve or create something
example: the university library is a fantastic resource
the country’s hospitals are not adequately resourced

definition: a row is a series of things or people in a straight line
example: you need to have three bricks of the same colour in a row
are these drugs safe?
local campaigns to improve road safety

definition: virtual means created by computer software, and appearing on computers or the internet
example: it’s a virtual world where you can create characters and stories

definition: an app is a piece of software that is designed for a particular purpose, especially one used on a tablet computer or mobile phone. App is short for application
example: a new dictionary app is now available

definition: to attack a person or a place means to try to hurt or damage them by using violence. An attacker is a person who attacks someone
example: the identity of the attacker remains unclear
a violent racist attack
a violent racist attack

definition: if a machine does something automatically, it has no human help
example: the machine does everything automatically
the automatic doors opened

definition: to build something in means to make it part of something else
example: the suitcase has GPS built in, so you can track it wherever it is
a built-in wardrobe

definition: if you edit a document, you correct mistakes and make changes so that it is ready to be published. If you edit a film or video, you arrange it in a particular order and take out the parts that you do not want
example: the software allows you to record, edit and share videos
only minor edits should be made to this page
excellent typing and editing skills are necessary
a magazine/newspaper editor
the author has retained editorial control

definition: a gadget is a small machine or device which helps you do something useful
example: a clever kitchen gadget
it doesn’t rely solely on technical gadgetry

definition: an interface is the point in a computer system where information passes from the computer to the person using it
example: it’s got a very clear user interface

definition: used for adding emphasis to questions with what, why, where, etc, especially when the person asking the question is surprised, angry, or does not think there is an obvious answer
example: why on earth would you want to do that?

definition: a pillow is a soft object that you rest your head on when you are in bed
example: this is a really comfy pillow

definition: random means not following any particular method, pattern, or purpose
example: my friends James and Jodie review random stuff chosen by listeners
participants were randomly selected

definition: if you remind someone about something or remind them to do it, you help them remember to do it. A reminder is a letter or note that reminds you about something you need to remember to do
example: I’m bound to forget – can you send me a reminder?
can you remind me to phone dad?

definition: remote means capable of being operated from a distance, usually using an electronic link
example: use the remote control to change channels
a remotely controlled lighting system

definition: if a machine runs on or runs off a type of energy, it uses that type of energy
example: we can run the lighting off the mains (the electricity that comes into the house from outside)

definition: scared means frightened. If you scare someone off, you make them so frightened that they go away. You can also say scare someone away
example: the cry for help is supposed to scare off attackers in the street

definition: a sensor is a piece of equipment that reacts to physical changes such as heat or light
example: it has a built-in sensor that can read the temperature

definition: if you smash something or it smashes, it breaks into many pieces by being dropped or hit
example: the ball flew across the garden and smashed the window

definition: solar means coming from or relating to the sun
example: the whole house runs on solar power

definition: a timer is a piece of equipment that measures time, especially one that makes a machine start or stop working at a particular time
example: remember to set the timer so that the heating comes on

definition: a torch is a small electric light that is powered by batteries and which you hold in your hand. The American word is flashlight
example: a powerful torch

definition: if you track someone or something, you use a special device to follow their movements
example: the suitcase has GPS built in, so you can track it wherever it is

definition: something that is user-friendly is easy for people to use or understand
example: the website is very user-friendly
translation: удобный для пользователя, простой в использовании

example: the user-friendliness of the site will definitely appeal to many

definition: a condition is a long-term illness or health problem
example: he’s got an incurable medical condition

definition: data is facts or information that can be analysed and used for making calculations or decisions
example: none of the personal data collected is ever passed on

definition: your lungs are the two organs inside your chest that fill with air when you breathe
example: one serious side effect is a collapsed lung

definition: if someone or something reacts to something, they behave in a particular way because of it
example: how did he react when you told him?
collocations: react to something Collocates:  a reaction to something

definition: a sample is a small amount of a substance that is used for scientific analysis
example: the GPS tracking system marks where each sample of air was taken
they were carefully sampled for further analysis in a laboratory
